jokes.text: Why do you shave and Ashley doesn't?
jokes.setup: Innocent child asking about Will's shaving cut
jokes.punchline: Kid notices gender differences in shaving habits with uncomfortable directness
jokes.explanation: Strong kid logic moment - innocently cuts to uncomfortable truth about gender differences

jokes.text: Will's stammering explanation about guys growing beards and women growing... taller
jokes.setup: Will starts to explain puberty differences between boys and girls
jokes.punchline: Ashley saves him by suggesting 'taller' when he clearly was heading somewhere inappropriate
jokes.explanation: Well-executed awkward moment with smart character teamwork to avoid inappropriate territory

jokes.text: And some vessels just stay empty
jokes.setup: Phil's philosophical comment about Nicky being 'an empty vessel waiting to be filled with knowledge'
jokes.punchline: Immediate cut to Hilary asking about her old magazine, Phil delivers the burn
jokes.explanation: Perfectly timed burn with excellent setup/payoff structure and character-specific delivery

jokes.text: Rich People's Digest
jokes.setup: Someone asks what magazine Hilary is reading for decorating advice
jokes.punchline: Made-up magazine title that perfectly captures Hilary's oblivious wealth
jokes.explanation: Perfect character-specific absurdity that reveals Hilary's mindset through fake magazine title

jokes.text: Yeah, if you're a hotel in Vegas
jokes.setup: Aunt Vivian suggests a color for drapes
jokes.punchline: Will's specific and cutting comparison to tacky Vegas hotel decor
jokes.explanation: Sharp, specific insult with good cultural reference point

jokes.text: Simple, Will, some of us are born owls and some of us are born mules. Guess which one you are.
jokes.setup: Will complains about carrying everything
jokes.punchline: Carlton's pretentious philosophical putdown comparing Will to a mule
jokes.explanation: Classic Carlton pretentiousness with animal metaphor perfectly in character

jokes.text: Well, I must be one of them owl-killing mules, then
jokes.setup: Carlton's owl/mule analogy
jokes.punchline: Will's street-smart comeback threatening Carlton
jokes.explanation: Clever callback that turns Carlton's metaphor into a threat, perfect Will/Carlton dynamic

jokes.text: Them arches better not be golden
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil mentions camping at 'Two Arches'
jokes.punchline: Will's McDonald's reference about golden arches
jokes.explanation: Solid pun playing on McDonald's golden arches vs. natural rock formations

jokes.text: Great, I'm gonna go stuff my fanny pack
jokes.setup: Phil talks about roughing it like frontiersmen
jokes.punchline: Immediately mentions his fanny pack, destroying any rugged image
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast between Phil's frontiersman fantasy and suburban reality

jokes.text: Looks like somebody beat him to it
jokes.setup: Phil mentions stuffing his fanny pack
jokes.punchline: Will's fat joke about Phil's already-stuffed posterior
jokes.explanation: Classic fat joke setup but well-timed and economical delivery

jokes.text: I tried to tell puny the bear this is too much stuff
jokes.setup: Will commenting on all the camping equipment
jokes.punchline: Calling Carlton 'puny the bear' - ironic size reference
jokes.explanation: Creative nickname that plays on Carlton's size while referencing camping/bear theme

jokes.text: I mean, come on, why are we taking wood? / Hello? Campfire?
jokes.setup: Will questions why they're bringing wood camping
jokes.punchline: Carlton's condescending 'Hello? Campfire?' as if Will is stupid
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton condescension moment. The 'Hello?' delivery is very characteristic of his know-it-all attitude.

jokes.text: How many Uncle Phils?
jokes.setup: Carlton says the raft seats six people
jokes.punchline: Will questions capacity using Uncle Phil as oversized measurement unit
jokes.explanation: Clever use of Phil as a unit of measurement, perfectly economical and quotable

jokes.text: There was a raft in that box
jokes.setup: Carlton warns not to pull the cord, Will pulls it anyway, raft inflates
jokes.punchline: Will's stunned realization delivered deadpan
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy payoff with Will's characteristic deadpan reaction

jokes.text: It had a built-in bug zapper
jokes.setup: Arguing about the cost of a broken hurricane lamp
jokes.punchline: Carlton reveals the lamp had an absurd additional feature
jokes.explanation: Absurd product feature that explains high price, fits Carlton's over-preparation

jokes.text: The rapid-fire price negotiation ending with 'You got a deal, Daffy'
jokes.setup: Back-and-forth haggling between Will and Carlton
jokes.punchline: Will accidentally agrees to the higher price, Carlton calls him Daffy Duck
jokes.explanation: Classic negotiation reversal with cartoon reference for the confused party

jokes.text: Well, those white sheets can get so dingy. Don't you have any nice pastels?
jokes.setup: Family is worried about Will in the hospital
jokes.punchline: Hilary worries about the aesthetics of hospital bedding
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary moment - completely missing the gravity while focusing on decor

jokes.text: Well, what about television? He's not gonna just have basic cable, is he?
jokes.setup: Nurse tries to manage Hilary's inappropriate concerns
jokes.punchline: Hilary continues with cable package concerns for shooting victim
jokes.explanation: Escalates Hilary's misplaced priorities, perfectly in character

jokes.text: And what is Prozac anyway? I mean, do you think I need this?
jokes.setup: Hilary asking about medication given to her
jokes.punchline: Completely oblivious to why she might need anti-anxiety medication
jokes.explanation: Character-consistent obliviousness but somewhat predictable

jokes.text: However, I will be doing my banking by mail from now on
jokes.setup: Will reassuring everyone he's tough
jokes.punchline: Immediately admits he's avoiding ATMs
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - bravado immediately undercut by practical fear

jokes.text: Knowing my luck, I'll run into a disgruntled postal worker
jokes.setup: Will mentioning banking by mail
jokes.punchline: Reference to postal worker violence stereotype
jokes.explanation: Dark humor callback to '90s postal violence fears, good follow-up

jokes.text: Well, it ain't as bad as the last time Geoffrey made chili
jokes.setup: Phil asks if Will is in pain
jokes.punchline: Will compares gunshot pain to Geoffrey's bad cooking
jokes.explanation: Clever comparison that maintains Will's humor while acknowledging Geoffrey

jokes.text: I hate this generic stuff. I'm gonna get you an Evian
jokes.setup: Will makes a joke about Geoffrey's chili
jokes.punchline: Hilary completely misses context and focuses on upgrading hospital water
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary non-sequitur showing her priorities even in crisis

jokes.text: I'm scared to touch anything. I don't wanna pull anything out
jokes.setup: Will asks why Ashley is staying far away
jokes.punchline: Ashley's innocent but suggestive explanation
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment with unintentional double entendre

jokes.text: Psych.
jokes.setup: Will pretends to be hurt when Ashley finally hugs him
jokes.punchline: Reveals he was faking the pain to scare her
jokes.explanation: Classic Will move - can't resist a prank even in hospital. Good economy with just 'Psych.'

jokes.text: I did? I was just trying to get the hell out the way
jokes.setup: Carlton says Will took a bullet for him and saved his life
jokes.punchline: Will deflates the heroic narrative with honest self-preservation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Will honesty that deflates heroic mythology with street-smart realism

jokes.text: Don't tell me any more fairy tales
jokes.setup: Phil trying to reassure Carlton about the justice system
jokes.punchline: Carlton's bitter dismissal of his father's optimism
jokes.explanation: Powerful character moment showing Carlton's worldview shattered

jokes.text: Don't go peeking at my stuff, Mom
jokes.setup: Will's mom offers to help with bedpan situation
jokes.punchline: Will's embarrassed boundary-setting with his mother
jokes.explanation: Realistic family dynamics humor in awkward medical situation

jokes.text: I see there's a new pillow fluffer in town, huh?
jokes.setup: Will's mom is taking care of him, Geoffrey arrives
jokes.punchline: Will notices the hierarchy shift in caretaking
jokes.explanation: Good observation about family dynamics and service roles

jokes.text: Ow! Ooh, psych, psych, psych
jokes.setup: Ashley is leaving, another opportunity for Will to fake pain
jokes.punchline: Will does the same fake-out again
jokes.explanation: Running gag callback but diminishing returns on the same trick

jokes.text: Well, if it stops beeping, you're gonna sleep a lot longer than you planned
jokes.setup: Will complains about the beeping heart monitor keeping him awake
jokes.punchline: His mom's dark humor about what it means if the beeping stops
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor from Mom - unexpected source for death joke makes it funnier

jokes.text: Well, you know how they say a paper cut is the worst kind of pain? Eh! Eh! Wrong.
jokes.setup: Vy asks how Will feels
jokes.punchline: Will's comparison between paper cut mythology and gunshot reality
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor comparing minor vs. major pain with game show delivery

jokes.text: Did you hear the one about the black dude that got shot at the ATM?
jokes.setup: Vy tells Will he doesn't need to entertain her
jokes.punchline: Will starts a joke about his own situation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-humor where Will makes a joke about his own trauma, very sophisticated

jokes.text: Oh, no, this is What's Happening!! I know all these jokes by heart.
jokes.setup: Nurse asks if it's hard to follow the show with sound off
jokes.punchline: Will knows every joke in the classic Black sitcom
jokes.explanation: Good cultural reference that makes sense for Will's character and viewing habits

jokes.text: Oh, check it out, look, look, look. [IMITATES DWAYNE] Hey, hey, hey. [IMITATES DEE] Oh, Dwayne, Rerun ate my mama's cake. [IMITATES RERUN] Ooh. Dee, Dee, please don't tell your mama I ate her cake. [IMITATES DEE] Okay, Rerun, for Dee right price.
jokes.setup: Will demonstrating his knowledge of What's Happening!! episodes
jokes.punchline: His impressions of the characters and their dialogue patterns
jokes.explanation: Showcases Will's pop culture knowledge and impression skills. Good character moment though the comedy relies on recognition of the referenced show.

jokes.text: Oh, that ain't nothing. You should see my Webster.
jokes.setup: Nurse compliments Will's impressions
jokes.punchline: Will mentions his Webster impression
jokes.explanation: Follow-up cultural reference, solid but not as strong as the setup

jokes.text: What exactly do you want her to see?
jokes.setup: Will mentions his Webster
jokes.punchline: Mom's suggestive comeback about what Will wants to show the nurse
jokes.explanation: Sharp innuendo comeback from unexpected source, well-timed

jokes.text: You always dream about Shaka Zulu?
jokes.setup: Will was talking in his sleep
jokes.punchline: Mom reveals Will was having dreams about African warrior king
jokes.explanation: Unexpected cultural reference that fits Will's background and creates embarrassing revelation

jokes.text: No, I'll thank Jazz later
jokes.setup: Vy offers to read a card that came with flowers
jokes.punchline: Will immediately assumes tacky flowers are from Jazz
jokes.explanation: Good character knowledge - Will knows Jazz's taste level perfectly

jokes.text: See, even if you stood right in front of me... he still could have hit me from my belly button up
jokes.setup: Carlton saying Will saved his life by taking the bullet
jokes.punchline: Will demonstrates with height difference how Carlton wouldn't have blocked much
jokes.explanation: Excellent visual comedy using height difference, deflates heroic narrative with logic

jokes.text: Well, uh... you could talk to Uncle Phil about, you know, sliding me into his will. Forty, 50 G's, you know?
jokes.setup: Carlton says he owes Will anything he wants
jokes.punchline: Will immediately tries to monetize the life debt
jokes.explanation: Perfect Will opportunism - can't resist trying to cash in on emotional moment

jokes.text: A brother get shot, he get a little emotional
jokes.setup: Will asking Carlton for a hug
jokes.punchline: Will's justification for needing emotional support
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Will's need for connection masked as street logic

jokes.text: What are you gonna do with that? / It's for protection. / Carlton. Whoa, man. What, do you think it's that easy to just shoot somebody? / I'll close my eyes.
jokes.setup: Carlton reveals he's carrying a gun for protection, Will questions his ability to use it
jokes.punchline: Carlton's naive solution to killing someone is to just close his eyes
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment showing Carlton's complete naivety about violence even while armed. The childish logic of closing his eyes is perfectly Carlton.

jokes.text: I was gonna eat that, man
jokes.setup: Carlton accidentally shoots the jello
jokes.punchline: Will's mundane concern about his destroyed hospital food
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic timing - focuses on trivial loss amid serious gun revelation

jokes.text: That's not you, man, that's them
jokes.setup: Carlton explaining his new tough approach
jokes.punchline: Will's assessment that Carlton is adopting his attackers' mindset
jokes.explanation: Profound character insight delivered simply - shows Will's wisdom about identity

jokes.text: I saved your life, man. I saved your life. You owe me!
jokes.setup: Carlton refusing to give up the gun
jokes.punchline: Will desperately invoking the life debt he earlier dismissed
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback - Will finally claims the heroic narrative when he needs leverage
